itemized report which is prepared on a form prescribed by the
(Fail: political Practices) Commission and which provides the

“This title" is Title 9 of the Government code.
effective January 7, 1975 (see Govt. Code 591015),
campaign statements filed prior to January 7, 1975, or under
the provisions of law in effect prior to that time are not
"campaign statements" as defined in Government code ssznos.

could not have been prepared on forms prescribed by the Fair
Political Practices Commission, nor do they provide the informar

the date of the filing cf the statements in question.

reporting provisions of the political reform Act are invalidated

ered in full force and effect with respect to any activities

sections.conclusion: The retention and disposition of the statements about which you inquire, then, appears to be governed by the provisions of the Elections Code under which such statements were filed. Our response to your question must be limited to the application of the Political Reform Act to the issues you raise. For advice as to the requirements of the Elections Code or other law I would suggest you consult your city's corporation counsel, the county counsel, or the Attorney General.
sections.question_synthetic: May the City Clerk destroy campaign statements filed by municipal candidates prior to the January 7, 1975 effective date of the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign statements filed prior to January 7, 1975 are not governed by the Political Reform Act's preservation requirements, as they do not meet the Act's definition of "campaign statements." The retention and disposition of such pre-1975 statements are governed instead by the Elections Code provisions under which they were originally filed, requiring consultation with corporation counsel or the Attorney General for specific guidance.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with some legibility issues. Date header shows 'March 1A, 1950' which appears to be OCR error for 'March 14, 1980' based on referenced letter date of February 1, 1980. Question and conclusion sections clearly identifiable. Sender identified as John Giansello, Staff Services Analyst.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: s about requirements of the Political Reform Act, you may
I
call me at 916/322-5552.
I

CONCLUSION: The retention and disposition of the statements about which you inquire, then, appears to be governed by the provisions of the Elections Code under which such statements were filed. Our response to your question must be limited to the application of the Political Reform Act to the issues you raise. For advice as to the requirements of the Elections Code or other law I would suggest you consult your city's corporation counsel, the county counsel, or the Attorney General.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that campaign statements filed prior to January 7, 1975 (the effective date of the Political Reform Act) are not subject to the Act's preservation requirements since they do not meet the Act's definition of "campaign statements." The disposition of such statements is instead governed by Elections Code provisions in effect at the time they were filed.
